wine_museums.name: Mosel-Weinmuseum
wine_museums.description: Wine museum in the St. Nikolaus-Hospital cultural centre in Kues, tracing Mosel viticulture, slate terroir and winemaking history. Sits above the world's largest Mosel Riesling vinothek for a tasting after the visit.
wine_museums.admission: Museum entry with combined vinothek tasting tickets available

wine_museums.name: Mittelmosel-Museum
wine_museums.description: Local-history and wine-town museum in the baroque Villa Boecking, built around 1755 for the merchant Boecking family. More than 20 rooms trace Traben-Trarbach's wine-trade heyday, with period furniture and artworks.
wine_museums.admission: Open Easter to October, daily except Mondays, 10:00 to 17:00

wine_museums.name: Vereinigte Hospitien Historic Wine Cellar
wine_museums.description: The cellar of the Vereinigte Hospitien foundation estate, built around 330 AD and reckoned the oldest wine cellar in Germany. Guided tours present 1,700 years of Mosel and Saar cellaring under Roman vaults.
wine_museums.admission: Guided cellar tours Wednesdays and Fridays or by arrangement

wine_museums.name: Rheinisches Landesmuseum Trier (Neumagen Wine Ship)
wine_museums.description: State archaeology museum holding the Neumagen Wine Ship, a circa-220 AD Roman funerary monument of a Mosel wine merchant shaped as a barrel-laden ship. Centrepiece of Trier's Roman wine-trade heritage.
wine_museums.admission: Standard museum admission; see museum for current pricing
